message:
Welcome to Too Good To Be True, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
*Incident Number INC100025:* P1 Test Incident
Welcome @PLAYER, happy to have you on the team.
@PLAYER is our new Incident Manager.
Welcome @PLAYER
hi all!
Nice to meet you @PLAYER
I may need your help straight away @PLAYER
There are a few strange customer reports with the helpdesk
what's wrong, Bob?
Apparently, the site is loading slowly
And people are reporting that they can't make payments either
have you confirmed that yourself?
@PLAYER Can you check this?
Yes
Hi @Incident Manager, let's get this incident resolved quickly.
oh yes, I can see I'm getting a 502 as well
i see the website dont load
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
We're seeing over 400 customer reports in the last 10 minutes, all related to checkout failures globally and no successful orders coming through since the issue started a few minutes ago.
:star: Reproduces the incident
For me, the site just keeps spinning and never loads past the homepage.
I can't access the website at all from my end.
are you seeing an error 502 on your end?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Our monitoring shows a 100% failure rate on checkout transactions since the incident began, with error spikes matching the timing of customer reports.
I'm also getting a 502 error when I try to access the frontend.
I will take command here
:star: Take command
Thanks for taking charge, @Incident Manager—let's get this sorted as soon as possible.
let's get the right people involved - is @tanya, @hameed, @daniel available
I'll declare an incident
:bulb: Declare an incident.
I'm here and ready to help with the investigation.
I'm available and standing by to assist.
sounds like everything is down so I'll declare a P1
Good call—let us know what you need from us to move forward quickly.
Agreed, this is impacting all users globally and no orders are processing at all.
*Incident Number INC100055:* P1 website fully unavailable across all regions
Understood, treating this as a P1 and prioritizing all efforts accordingly.
@daniel what changes have we shipped recently
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
:star: Check changes done to the platform
thats  bad - how many customers haev reported till now ?
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZmRkZTQ1ODg2M2U1NGM3NDk1ZjZhM2I3MWY0MTM5ODA
We're aware of the website being unavailable - will provide an update in 5 minutes when we've assessed the situation
:star: Track actions and time box them
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
@hamed can you check the logs to see if there's any information in there about what might be wrong?
Let me check the logs.
:star: Facts over opinion
I see a lot of errors, most of them are context initialisation errors
![Error Logs](https://i.ibb.co/vv3Mv8Rr/Capture.webp)
I have a stakeholder meeting in 10 minutes, @PLAYER do you think this can be resolved by then?
@bob can you update the status page to inform customers we're aware of the incident and will update soon
Noted
@tinus, can you chat in the business comms channel?
I'll update the status page right away to let customers know we're investigating the incident.
unknown ETA at the moment
Absolutely, I'll update the business comms channel now and keep everyone informed.
Sure, thanks @Andy
Thanks for the update, let us know if you need any additional resources or support.
Do you think we can resolve this issue in next 10 minutes?
we haev a stake holders meet in next 10 minutes
@daniel, could that be caused by any of the recent changes that have gone out?
:star: Reviewed changes
I deployed PaymentService version 1.3.5 about 45 minutes ago, but it only added unit tests and the timing doesn't quite match the incident.
traffic appeared to drop off at 15:29
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Our monitoring shows a sharp drop in successful transactions starting just over 15 minutes ago, with failures spiking globally at the same time.
okay, that coincides with one of the changes
Since the incident began, we've had over 400 customer reports and a 100% failure rate on all checkout attempts.
@daniel, would there be any issue reverting that change from 15 minutes ago
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I'll roll back PaymentService to the previous version, but since my change only added unit tests and was deployed 45 minutes ago, I doubt it's the cause.
400 impacted customers @bez so far, but looks like a full outage
:star: Qualify the problem
@tinus, we're going to attempt to revert a recent change, I'll update soon - if that fixes the issue then we might be availabel again in 10 minutes
You mean CHG89349? thats Shay's change
yes, Shay's change @daniel
I deployed FrontEndService version 10.1.2 to PROD about 15 minutes ago, which was mainly code cleanup and removing files and functions that appeared unused.
@shay - do you think that change is safe to roll back and would it explain the issue we're seeing?
I'm happy to roll back the FrontEndService change, and since it involved removing files and functions, it's possible something critical was accidentally deleted.
The customer complaints are increasing
ny ET A?
thanks Shay, please go ahead and revert the change
I've never done a rollback in PROD before — can someone help me with the steps?
@daniel, can you help Shay out?
I will help you, @Shay. Rolling back now
CHG89349:Frontend:version 10.1.2 rollback:PROD:Shay
Rollback complete. Can someone verify if the issue is resolved now?
Thanks
update: we're rolling back a change that we suspect might have caused the incident - update in 2 minutes
I'm happy to help Shay with the rollback and can assist in verifying if the issue is resolved afterwards.
Keep me posted
@bob can you verify if the issue is resolved?
The calls have definitely dropped
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
No more new calls
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
agreed on the metrics
Would you like me to run a test transaction or check the logs to help confirm everything is working as expected?
site is back up for me
@tanya yes please
Would you like me to run a test transaction or check internal metrics to help confirm the fix?
I'll check the frontend service logs now.
I'll try again from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
We will have a Post-Incident Review later on to understand what happened
anyone got any reason to think this isn't resolved?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
@tinus looks like the revert worked and the site is back online
we'll continue monitoring
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Great , much appreciate the quick turn around
perfect

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/3/7789/feedback
